outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Iran missiles hit Dimona and Arad as IDF air defense fails — Netanyahu calls it "a very difficult evening"
outlets.summary: NPR documents the dual Iranian strikes on Arad and Dimona — where air defense failed at Arad, 74 injured — and the emerging US-Israel policy rift over war duration.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Air defense fails in Arad as Iran missile hits Dimona — raising questions about Israeli defense coverage
outlets.summary: CNN covers the Arad interception failure and Dimona strike as evidence that Iran retains significant strike capacity despite US claims of 90% degradation.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants if Hormuz not opened in 48 hours
outlets.summary: Trump's Truth Social ultimatum threatens civilian energy infrastructure for first time. IDF says campaign at 'halfway stage' with 3 more weeks planned. Iran fired missiles at Arad (74 injured) and Dimona (47 injured).

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Iran war enters Day 23: Trump's Hormuz deadline ticks down as Israel strikes Tehran R&D complex
outlets.summary: NPR covers the Hormuz deadline standoff — Trump giving Iran until Monday evening to fully reopen the strait or face strikes on power plants. Iran's refusal and counter-threat to Gulf desalination systems dominates the news cycle.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran fires on Israel's nuclear city and Arad — air defense fails — Netanyahu vows to "keep striking enemies"
outlets.summary: Fox News frames the Arad and Dimona strikes as continued Iranian aggression. Emphasizes Netanyahu resolve and IDF probe into air defense failure.

outlets.name: Wall Street Journal
outlets.headline: Israel-Iran war Day 22 PM: Iran missiles breach air defenses at Arad, Dimona — Houthis threaten Red Sea closure
outlets.summary: WSJ documents the dual strikes and the emerging Houthi Bab el-Mandeb threat, noting it could double the shipping crisis beyond Hormuz alone.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump gives Iran 48-hour ultimatum to reopen Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on power plants
outlets.summary: Trump posts blunt ultimatum on Truth Social. Fox praise the tough stance. Iran counter-threatens Gulf desalination. IDF: campaign at halfway stage, 3 more weeks.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Israel hits Tehran weapons lab on Day 23; Iran refuses Trump's Hormuz ultimatum — deadline Monday 7:44 PM ET
outlets.summary: Fox News covers Israel's Day 23 strike on the Malek Ashtar University nuclear/missile R&D complex and Iran's defiant rejection of Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum. Iran threatening Gulf desalination if power plants are struck.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran strikes Israeli nuclear city Dimona, Arad — Houthis weigh Bab el-Mandeb closure
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera emphasizes the civilian dimension of the Arad and Dimona strikes, the air defense failure, and the Houthi threat to close a second global chokepoint.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Iran missile hits Dimona, injuring dozens near Israel's nuclear site; IAEA says no damage to reactor
outlets.summary: Reuters provides factual coverage of both strikes, noting IAEA confirmation of no nuclear damage but highlighting the precedent of Iran targeting Israel's nuclear city.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war: Trump gives Iran 48-hour ultimatum as IDF says campaign at 'halfway stage'
outlets.summary: Trump's Hormuz ultimatum raises fears of expanded strikes on civilian infrastructure. Iran counter-threatens Gulf energy and water systems. IDF plans 3 more weeks of operations.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war Day 23: Israel strikes Tehran weapons lab; Iran rejects Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera focuses on the escalating Hormuz standoff and Netanyahu urging European leaders to join the military coalition against Iran. Israel struck East Tehran's Malek Ashtar University. Iran calls for 'complete and lasting end to war,' not a ceasefire.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Air defense fails in Arad — 74 hurt; Iran strikes Dimona; IDF opens probe
outlets.summary: Times of Israel live coverage of the Arad interception failure and Dimona strike. IDF probe opened. Netanyahu: "a very difficult evening."

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Iran hits Israeli nuclear city Dimona, breaches Arad air defense — Day 22 most dangerous evening of war
outlets.summary: JPost calls Day 22 evening the most dangerous night of the war so far — dual strikes on Arad and Dimona reveal sustained Iranian ballistic missile capability.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF Chief: Iran campaign at halfway stage; 3 more weeks planned — Katz says attacks intensifying
outlets.summary: Zamir: ~70% of Iran's missile launchers and ~80% of air defenses destroyed. 3 more weeks. Trump ultimatum welcome in Israel. Iran strikes Arad and Dimona (air defense failure).

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF strikes Malek Ashtar nuclear R&D in Tehran; Netanyahu at Arad demands Europe join war
outlets.summary: Times of Israel covers Day 23 IDF strikes on Tehran's main weapons university and Netanyahu's call for European intervention at Arad — the site struck by an Iranian IRBM on Day 22.

outlets.name: IRNA (Iran)
outlets.headline: Iran responds to Natanz attack with precision strikes on Israeli nuclear city Dimona and Arad
outlets.summary: Iranian state media frames the Arad and Dimona strikes as a proportional response to the US-Israeli strike on Natanz, emphasizing Iran's continued deterrence capability.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran's missiles pierce Israel's missile shield — strikes on Dimona, Arad prove Iran's power
outlets.summary: Press TV celebrates the Arad air defense failure and Dimona strike as proof that Iran retains a credible deterrence posture despite 22 days of US-Israeli strikes.

outlets.name: Press TV / Fars News
outlets.headline: Iran warns: US energy and desalination infrastructure across Gulf will be targeted if Iran's power grid is struck
outlets.summary: Iranian military's formal counter-threat to Trump's ultimatum. Iran claims Dimona and Arad strikes are 'proven capability.' Araghchi: Iran seeks 'complete and lasting end to war,' not a ceasefire.

outlets.name: IRNA (Iran)
outlets.headline: Iran military: Hormuz permanently closed if US strikes power plants; Araghchi demands end to all fronts
outlets.summary: Iranian state media emphasizes Iran's counter-threat to close Hormuz indefinitely and Araghchi's rejection of any ceasefire, framing the conflict as one requiring 'complete and lasting peace' with reparations — not a temporary halt.
